name: Model cards / system cards published
positive_direction_rule: New model release ships with substantive model card (training data, evals, limitations).
negative_direction_rule: Release without card, or cards that are marketing documents lacking technical substance.

name: Public evaluation results
positive_direction_rule: Labs publish benchmarks, red-team findings, dangerous-capability evals.
negative_direction_rule: Results withheld, cherry-picked, or replaced with self-attestation.

name: Capability disclosure at point of interaction
positive_direction_rule: Users told what model, its limitations, confidence bounds.
negative_direction_rule: AI systems deployed without disclosure, or disguised as humans.

name: Pre-deployment evaluation mandates
positive_direction_rule: Binding requirements to evaluate frontier models before release (capability, misuse, alignment).
negative_direction_rule: Mandates repealed, narrowed, or exemptions expanded.

name: Mandatory incident reporting
positive_direction_rule: Regulated actors required to report AI incidents to authorities within defined windows.
negative_direction_rule: Reporting rules relaxed or rescinded.

name: Third-party audit requirements
positive_direction_rule: Independent audits required for high-risk AI systems; right-of-access for auditors.
negative_direction_rule: Audits made voluntary, weakened, or captured by industry.

name: Red-team disclosure rules
positive_direction_rule: Binding red-team findings disclosure (to regulators, to public, or both).
negative_direction_rule: Red-team results kept confidential or never required.

name: Public expectation of transparency on AI capabilities
positive_direction_rule: Rising public/media/expert demand for visibility into AI training, evaluation, and deployment.
negative_direction_rule: Normalization of opacity; AI is too complex to explain framings gaining traction.
major_threshold: Coverage in 2+ tier-1 outlets same week; major policy coalition letter.

name: Open publication of safety evaluations as industry norm
positive_direction_rule: Labs publish eval suites, red-team findings, limitations openly.
negative_direction_rule: Withdrawal from voluntary transparency (removed system cards, deleted benchmarks).

name: Incident disclosure as expected behavior
positive_direction_rule: Near-misses, failures, jailbreaks disclosed proactively.
negative_direction_rule: Incidents concealed, NDAs on safety findings, whistleblower retaliation.

name: Safety-by-design practices
positive_direction_rule: Red teaming, threat modeling, safety evals integrated pre-release.
negative_direction_rule: Safety teams cut, evals skipped for shipping speed.

name: User reporting / abuse channels
positive_direction_rule: Functional reporting channels acted on; published response times and resolution rates.
negative_direction_rule: Reports ignored, channels removed, trust and safety teams dismantled.

name: Post-deployment monitoring and rapid response
positive_direction_rule: Labs monitor deployed systems, respond to emergent harms within defined windows.
negative_direction_rule: Ship and forget; no feedback loop from deployment to model updates.

name: Liability for foreseeable AI harms
positive_direction_rule: Courts or legislatures assign liability to AI developers/deployers for predictable damage.
negative_direction_rule: Section-230-style shields extended to AI output.
major_threshold: Supreme court ruling, federal statute, EU directive.

name: Duty-of-care statutes applied to AI
positive_direction_rule: Explicit duty-of-care obligations on AI developers/deployers.
negative_direction_rule: Carve-outs from existing duty-of-care frameworks.

name: Consumer protection enforcement against deceptive AI claims
positive_direction_rule: FTC/regulator enforcement against false AI claims, dark patterns, deceptive marketing.
negative_direction_rule: Enforcement declined, agencies defunded, no action despite complaints.

name: Private right of action / class action enablement
positive_direction_rule: Individuals can sue AI firms for harms; class actions viable.
negative_direction_rule: Mandatory arbitration, pre-emption, standing doctrine shutting out claims.

name: Professional standards and codes of conduct
positive_direction_rule: ML/AI engineering codes of ethics, professional licensing discussions gaining traction.
negative_direction_rule: Industry pushes move-fast-and-break-things framing; dismissal of responsibility.

name: Public expectation of company accountability for AI harms
positive_direction_rule: Mainstream expectation that AI companies are responsible for foreseeable downstream harms.
negative_direction_rule: Normalization of user-is-responsible / just-a-tool deflection.

name: Researcher and whistleblower protections as norm
positive_direction_rule: Strong norm that safety researchers inside labs can publish critical findings.
negative_direction_rule: Chilling effects — non-disparagement NDAs, retaliation, equity clawbacks.

name: Opt-out and de-personalization defaults
positive_direction_rule: Products ship with de-personalized defaults or easy toggles.
negative_direction_rule: Personalization forced, opt-out buried or impossible.

name: Well-being features shipped by default
positive_direction_rule: Time limits, break prompts, default quiet hours enabled out of the box.
negative_direction_rule: Features exist but hidden, disabled by default, or removed.

name: Attention respect in UX
positive_direction_rule: Fewer interruptive notifications; batch delivery; user-controlled attention.
negative_direction_rule: Notification aggressiveness increases; attention engineered for maximum capture.

name: Restrictions on dark patterns and manipulative UX
positive_direction_rule: Binding restrictions on specific dark patterns.
negative_direction_rule: Proposed rules dropped, enforcement rare, industry self-regulation accepted.

name: Protections for minors
positive_direction_rule: Age-appropriate design codes, default protections for under-18 users, content restrictions.
negative_direction_rule: Minor-protection rules weakened, pre-empted, or successfully challenged.

name: Ad / recommendation system transparency
positive_direction_rule: Regulation requiring recommendation-system auditability and user control.
negative_direction_rule: Algorithmic opacity protected as trade secret.

name: Public discourse on well-being metrics over engagement
positive_direction_rule: Rising critique of engagement-maximization; mainstream coverage of time-well-spent.
negative_direction_rule: Engagement metrics defended as proxy for value; critiques marginalized.

name: Critique of dark-pattern and addictive design
positive_direction_rule: Civil society, academics, regulators naming specific harmful patterns.
negative_direction_rule: Dark patterns rebranded as UX optimization.

name: Mental health implications in mainstream discourse
positive_direction_rule: Mainstream reporting on AI effects on attention, loneliness, cognitive development.
negative_direction_rule: Topic marginalized as moral panic.

name: Human-in-the-loop for consequential decisions
positive_direction_rule: Product design keeps humans in authoritative roles for high-stakes decisions.
negative_direction_rule: Full automation in high-stakes contexts without review.

name: Augmentation-over-replacement framing
positive_direction_rule: Products positioned as augmenting human work, with measurable augmentation outcomes.
negative_direction_rule: Products explicitly marketed as replacing human workers.

name: Transparent attribution of AI-generated work
positive_direction_rule: AI-generated outputs clearly labeled; provenance tools deployed.
negative_direction_rule: AI work passed off as human; labeling avoided.

name: Worker displacement protections and transition funding
positive_direction_rule: Binding transition support, retraining obligations tied to AI deployment.
negative_direction_rule: Displacement externalized onto workers; no safety net expansion.

name: Algorithmic management regulations
positive_direction_rule: Rules limiting algorithmic supervision, scheduling, and discipline of workers.
negative_direction_rule: Algorithmic management expands unregulated.

name: Notification requirements before AI deployment in workplace
positive_direction_rule: Employers required to notify and consult workers before deploying consequential AI.
negative_direction_rule: No notification duty; workers learn of AI via effects.

name: Automated decision-making rights
positive_direction_rule: Rights to explanation, human review, and contestation of consequential automated decisions.
negative_direction_rule: Automated decisions opaque and uncontestable.

name: Public discourse on AI and labor
positive_direction_rule: Nuanced public conversation on displacement, augmentation, and worker power.
negative_direction_rule: Binary AI-will-replace-X rhetoric dominates without worker-voice balance.

name: Worker voice in AI deployment decisions
positive_direction_rule: Union position statements, works councils, collective bargaining over AI deployment.
negative_direction_rule: Workers systematically excluded from deployment decisions.

name: Public skepticism toward AI replacement rhetoric
positive_direction_rule: Mainstream pushback against inevitabilist framings.
negative_direction_rule: Replacement treated as inevitable, debate shut down.

name: Privacy-preserving design defaults
positive_direction_rule: Minimization defaults, local/on-device processing, differential privacy shipped.
negative_direction_rule: Maximum data capture, centralized processing by default.

name: Bias testing and fairness tooling in development
positive_direction_rule: Fairness audits as standard practice; published mitigation results.
negative_direction_rule: Fairness work deprioritized or eliminated.

name: User rights surfaced in UX
positive_direction_rule: Access, correction, deletion surfaced as first-class UI.
negative_direction_rule: Rights exist on paper but buried or dark-patterned.

name: Biometric and facial recognition limits
positive_direction_rule: Bans or binding limits on law-enforcement or commercial use.
negative_direction_rule: Expansions of use, rollbacks of moratoria.
major_threshold: Federal, EU AI Act, state-level bans.

name: Algorithmic bias and discrimination protections
positive_direction_rule: Anti-discrimination laws explicitly cover algorithmic decision-making.
negative_direction_rule: Algorithmic exceptions to existing anti-discrimination law.

name: Data protection strengthening
positive_direction_rule: New/expanded privacy rights (access, portability, deletion, purpose limitation).
negative_direction_rule: Weakening or pre-emption of state privacy laws.

name: Restrictions on predictive policing and algorithmic sentencing
positive_direction_rule: Bans or oversight regimes on these systems.
negative_direction_rule: Expansion without oversight.

name: Public debate on AI surveillance and civil liberties
positive_direction_rule: Journalism, civil society sustained pressure on surveillance applications.
negative_direction_rule: Surveillance normalized as security or convenience.

name: Attention to algorithmic harms
positive_direction_rule: Investigations, exposes on discriminatory outcomes, algorithmic bias.
negative_direction_rule: Harms under-reported; bias-is-solved narrative gains.

name: Recognition of algorithmic discrimination
positive_direction_rule: Mainstream acknowledgment that AI systems can systematically disadvantage groups.
negative_direction_rule: Framing shifts to AI-is-neutral-humans-are-biased.

name: Voluntary industry safety commitments
positive_direction_rule: Frontier labs publish specific, testable safety commitments.
negative_direction_rule: Commitments withdrawn, watered down, or shown to be unmet.

name: Information sharing on dangerous capabilities
positive_direction_rule: Labs share capability evaluations with peers and regulators via defined channels.
negative_direction_rule: Competitive secrecy prevails; no structured sharing.

name: Evaluation protocols aligned internationally
positive_direction_rule: Cross-lab and cross-border eval methodology converging.
negative_direction_rule: Proliferation of idiosyncratic self-evals that are not comparable.

name: Multilateral treaties and conventions
positive_direction_rule: New binding multilateral instruments; ratifications.
negative_direction_rule: Withdrawals, stalled negotiations, unsigned protocols.
major_threshold: Council of Europe AI Convention, G7/G20 binding commitments.

name: Export controls on frontier compute and models
positive_direction_rule: Export controls targeting dangerous-capability compute, coordinated across jurisdictions.
negative_direction_rule: Controls relaxed, loopholes exploited, uncoordinated.

name: Compute governance and licensing
positive_direction_rule: Frontier-compute licensing regimes, thresholds codified.
negative_direction_rule: Proposals shelved; opposition from industry successful.

name: Safety thresholds codified internationally
positive_direction_rule: Shared red-line definitions (bioweapon uplift, cyber offense, autonomy thresholds).
negative_direction_rule: No shared definitions; racing to ship past unstated thresholds.

name: International scientific consensus on risks
positive_direction_rule: IPCC-for-AI-style bodies, published risk assessments, expert consensus statements.
negative_direction_rule: Consensus processes stall; risk denial gains.

name: Multilateral civil society coalitions
positive_direction_rule: Cross-border coalitions coordinating on AI governance demands.
negative_direction_rule: Fragmentation, national retreat.

name: Public expectation of cross-border governance
positive_direction_rule: Mainstream recognition that AI risks require international coordination.
negative_direction_rule: Techno-nationalism dominant.

name: Open-source model releases
positive_direction_rule: Open-weights releases of frontier-class models, with responsible-release practices.
negative_direction_rule: Frontier models universally closed; open releases withdrawn or restricted.

name: Decentralized and federated architectures
positive_direction_rule: Federated training, on-device inference, decentralized serving growing.
negative_direction_rule: Centralization accelerates; only incumbents can serve frontier models.

name: Third-party access to closed models
positive_direction_rule: API parity for third-party developers vs internal teams; fair access terms.
negative_direction_rule: Internal-first APIs, discriminatory pricing, sudden access revocation.

name: Antitrust action against AI market concentration
positive_direction_rule: Merger challenges, structural remedies, conduct rules applied to AI firms.
negative_direction_rule: Mergers waved through; investigations dropped.

name: Interoperability and data portability mandates
positive_direction_rule: Requirements for data portability, model interoperability, API access on fair terms.
negative_direction_rule: Walled gardens protected; DMA-style rules weakened.

name: Public-option AI and sovereign compute funding
positive_direction_rule: Public investment in open infrastructure, academic compute, non-commercial alternatives.
negative_direction_rule: Public AI starved; private incumbents dominant.

name: Restrictions on political uses of AI
positive_direction_rule: Rules on deepfakes in campaigns, AI-generated political ads, disinformation.
negative_direction_rule: No rules; AI-manipulated political speech unregulated.

name: Antitrust and competition discourse applied to AI
positive_direction_rule: Mainstream conversation about AI market concentration and vertical integration risks.
negative_direction_rule: National-champion framing dominates; concentration celebrated.

name: Open-source vs closed-source debate
positive_direction_rule: Substantive public debate; neither side monopolizes framing.
negative_direction_rule: Debate captured — either open-sourcing dismissed as dangerous, or closed AI dismissed as illegitimate, without serious engagement.

name: Concerns about democratic capture
positive_direction_rule: Civil society surfaces AI-firm influence on policy; lobbying scrutinized.
negative_direction_rule: Regulatory capture normalized; AI firms set the agenda unchallenged.
